1вҶ’# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist вҖ” `deepmind/718.lean`
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4вҶ’without an explicit justification.
     5вҶ’
     6вҶ’---
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’## 1. Copyright Header
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
    11вҶ’- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’## 2. Import Statement
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
    16вҶ’- [x] No other imports are present.
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’## 3. Module Docstring
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    21вҶ’- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdЕ‘s Problem 718`.
    22вҶ’- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/718`.
    23вҶ’- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Dirac's result on KвӮ„ subdivisions, Mader's bound, and the ErdЕ‘sвҖ“HajnalвҖ“Mader conjecture on the cВ·rВІВ·n threshold).
    24вҶ’- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[KoSz96]` (KomlГіsвҖ“SzemerГ©di) and `[BoTh96]` (BollobГЎsвҖ“Thomason).
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’## 4. Namespace
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos718 ... end Erdos718`.
    29вҶ’- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos718`.
    30вҶ’- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’## 5. Custom Definitions
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’- [x] `ContainsSubdivision` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing what it formalizes.
    35вҶ’- [x] No `noncomputable` needed вҖ” definition is a `Prop`-valued existential using only constructive operations.
    36вҶ’- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    37вҶ’- [x] `ContainsSubdivision` uses `UpperCamelCase` (Prop-valued, so this is correct per convention).
    38вҶ’- [x] No `local notation` needed (no problem-specific notation introduced). N/A.
    39вҶ’- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for `ContainsSubdivision`. **Optional** вҖ” the checklist says "consider adding" rather than requiring. Acceptable to omit.
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’## 6. Theorem Statements
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    44вҶ’- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_718` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    45вҶ’- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention for Prop terms.
    46вҶ’- [x] Has both `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` on the **same** attribute line.
    47вҶ’- [x] Theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) referencing `[KoSz96]` and `[BoTh96]`.
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’### `answer()` usage
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’The problem is stated as a direct assertion: "There exists an absolute constant C > 0 such that any graph on n vertices with at least CВ·rВІВ·n edges contains a subdivision of K_r." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion).
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’- [x] Theorem type is `вҲғ C : в„қ, C > 0 вҲ§ вҲҖ ...` directly вҖ” no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
    54вҶ’- [x] No misplaced quantifiers as theorem parameters (all quantifiers are in the theorem body after `вҲғ C`).
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’## 7. Category Attribute
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    59вҶ’- [x] Value is `research solved` вҖ” valid category. The problem was proved by KomlГіsвҖ“SzemerГ©di and BollobГЎsвҖ“Thomason but not formally verified in Lean, so `research solved` is the correct classification (not `research formally solved`).
    60вҶ’- [x] Not `research open`, so no constraint on `sorry` presence. The `sorry` in the proof body is expected and acceptable for `research solved`.
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’## 8. AMS Attribute
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
    65вҶ’- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
    66вҶ’- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number вҖ” trivially satisfied).
    67вҶ’- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    68вҶ’- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (present in the valid code list).
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’## 9. Proof Bodies
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` вҖ” acceptable multi-line form per checklist.
    73вҶ’- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    74вҶ’- [x] No informal proof sketches for the solved research problem.
    75вҶ’- [x] No `native_decide`.
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’- N/A вҖ” no variants present. The file formalizes a single main conjecture.
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’## 11. Code Quality
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    84вҶ’- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `вҲҖ`, `вҲғ`, `вҲҲ`, `вҲү`, `вҲ§`, `вүҘ`, `вү `, `в„қ`, `в„•`, `вҶӘ`.
    85вҶ’- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    86вҶ’- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    87вҶ’- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    88вҶ’- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’## 12. Build Verification
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’- [ ] `lake build` вҖ” not verified in this review (requires build environment).
    93вҶ’- [ ] All five custom linters pass вҖ” not verified in this review.
    94вҶ’
    95вҶ’---
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’**Summary:** The file is fully compliant with all applicable checklist items. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, definition, theorem statement, category/AMS attributes, answer() usage (Case 3 вҖ” no wrapper needed), proof body, and code quality all conform to the style guidelines. The only unchecked items are build verification (requires running `lake build`) and the optional suggestion to add `@[category test]` sanity checks for the `ContainsSubdivision` definition.

# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist вҖ” `deepmind/718.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdЕ‘s Problem 718`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/718`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Dirac's result on KвӮ„ subdivisions, Mader's bound, and the ErdЕ‘sвҖ“HajnalвҖ“Mader conjecture on the cВ·rВІВ·n threshold).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[KoSz96]` (KomlГіsвҖ“SzemerГ©di) and `[BoTh96]` (BollobГЎsвҖ“Thomason).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos718 ... end Erdos718`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos718`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `ContainsSubdivision` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing what it formalizes.
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed вҖ” definition is a `Prop`-valued existential using only constructive operations.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `ContainsSubdivision` uses `UpperCamelCase` (Prop-valued, so this is correct per convention).
- [x] No `local notation` needed (no problem-specific notation introduced). N/A.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for `ContainsSubdivision`. **Optional** вҖ” the checklist says "consider adding" rather than requiring. Acceptable to omit.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_718` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention for Prop terms.
- [x] Has both `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` on the **same** attribute line.
- [x] Theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) referencing `[KoSz96]` and `[BoTh96]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is stated as a direct assertion: "There exists an absolute constant C > 0 such that any graph on n vertices with at least CВ·rВІВ·n edges contains a subdivision of K_r." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion).

- [x] Theorem type is `вҲғ C : в„қ, C > 0 вҲ§ вҲҖ ...` directly вҖ” no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
- [x] No misplaced quantifiers as theorem parameters (all quantifiers are in the theorem body after `вҲғ C`).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` вҖ” valid category. The problem was proved by KomlГіsвҖ“SzemerГ©di and BollobГЎsвҖ“Thomason but not formally verified in Lean, so `research solved` is the correct classification (not `research formally solved`).
- [x] Not `research open`, so no constraint on `sorry` presence. The `sorry` in the proof body is expected and acceptable for `research solved`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number вҖ” trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (present in the valid code list).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` вҖ” acceptable multi-line form per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the solved research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A вҖ” no variants present. The file formalizes a single main conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `вҲҖ`, `вҲғ`, `вҲҲ`, `вҲү`, `вҲ§`, `вүҘ`, `вү `, `в„қ`, `в„•`, `вҶӘ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` вҖ” not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] All five custom linters pass вҖ” not verified in this review.

---

**Summary:** The file is fully compliant with all applicable checklist items. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, definition, theorem statement, category/AMS attributes, answer() usage (Case 3 вҖ” no wrapper needed), proof body, and code quality all conform to the style guidelines. The only unchecked items are build verification (requires running `lake build`) and the optional suggestion to add `@[category test]` sanity checks for the `ContainsSubdivision` definition.

     2вҶ’Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3вҶ’

     7вҶ’

     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11вҶ’distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12вҶ’WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13вҶ’See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14вҶ’limitations under the License.
    15вҶ’-/
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 718
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/718](https://www.erdosproblems.com/718)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’A conjecture of ErdЕ‘s, Hajnal, and Mader. Dirac proved that every graph on $n$ vertices
    25вҶ’with at least $2n - 2$ edges contains a subdivision of $K_4$, and conjectured that $3n - 5$
    26вҶ’edges forces a subdivision of $K_5$.
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’Mader proved that $\geq 2^{\binom{r}{2}} \cdot n$ edges suffices.
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’[KoSz96] KomlГіs, J. and SzemerГ©di, E., _Topological cliques in graphs II_. Combinatorics,
    31вҶ’Probability and Computing (1996), 5, 79вҖ“90.
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’[BoTh96] BollobГЎs, B. and Thomason, A., _Proof of a conjecture of Mader, ErdЕ‘s and Hajnal
    34вҶ’on topological complete subgraphs_. European J. Combin. (1998), 19, 883вҖ“887.
    35вҶ’-/
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’open SimpleGraph
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’namespace Erdos718
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’/-- A graph $G$ contains a subdivision of $K_k$ if there exist $k$ distinct branch vertices
    42вҶ’and internally vertex-disjoint paths between every pair of branch vertices. -/
    43вҶ’def ContainsSubdivision {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V]
    44вҶ’    (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : в„•) : Prop :=
    45вҶ’  вҲғ (f : Fin k вҶӘ V)
    46вҶ’    (paths : вҲҖ (i j : Fin k), i < j вҶ’ G.Walk (f i) (f j)),
    47вҶ’    (вҲҖ i j (h : i < j), (paths i j h).IsPath) вҲ§
    48вҶ’    (вҲҖ iвӮҒ jвӮҒ (hвӮҒ : iвӮҒ < jвӮҒ) iвӮӮ jвӮӮ (hвӮӮ : iвӮӮ < jвӮӮ),
    49вҶ’      (iвӮҒ, jвӮҒ) вү  (iвӮӮ, jвӮӮ) вҶ’
    50вҶ’      вҲҖ v, v вҲҲ (paths iвӮҒ jвӮҒ hвӮҒ).support.tail.dropLast вҶ’
    51вҶ’           v вҲү (paths iвӮӮ jвӮӮ hвӮӮ).support.tail.dropLast) вҲ§
    52вҶ’    (вҲҖ i j (h : i < j) v,
    53вҶ’      v вҲҲ (paths i j h).support.tail.dropLast вҶ’
    54вҶ’      вҲҖ m : Fin k, v вү  f m)
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’/--
    57вҶ’**ErdЕ‘s Problem 718** (KomlГіsвҖ“SzemerГ©di [KoSz96], BollobГЎsвҖ“Thomason [BoTh96]):
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’There exists an absolute constant $C > 0$ such that any graph on $n$ vertices
    60вҶ’with at least $C \cdot r^2 \cdot n$ edges contains a subdivision of $K_r$.
    61вҶ’-/
    62вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    63вҶ’theorem erdos_718 :
    64вҶ’    вҲғ C : в„қ, C > 0 вҲ§
    65вҶ’      вҲҖ (n r : в„•),
    66вҶ’        вҲҖ (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    67вҶ’          (G.edgeFinset.card : в„қ) вүҘ C * (r : в„қ) ^ 2 * (n : в„қ) вҶ’
    68вҶ’          ContainsSubdivision G r := by
    69вҶ’  sorry
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’end Erdos718
    72вҶ’

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdЕ‘s Problem 718

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/718](https://www.erdosproblems.com/718)

A conjecture of ErdЕ‘s, Hajnal, and Mader. Dirac proved that every graph on $n$ vertices
with at least $2n - 2$ edges contains a subdivision of $K_4$, and conjectured that $3n - 5$
edges forces a subdivision of $K_5$.

Mader proved that $\geq 2^{\binom{r}{2}} \cdot n$ edges suffices.

[KoSz96] KomlГіs, J. and SzemerГ©di, E., _Topological cliques in graphs II_. Combinatorics,
Probability and Computing (1996), 5, 79вҖ“90.

[BoTh96] BollobГЎs, B. and Thomason, A., _Proof of a conjecture of Mader, ErdЕ‘s and Hajnal
on topological complete subgraphs_. European J. Combin. (1998), 19, 883вҖ“887.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos718

/-- A graph $G$ contains a subdivision of $K_k$ if there exist $k$ distinct branch vertices
and internally vertex-disjoint paths between every pair of branch vertices. -/
def ContainsSubdivision {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V]
    (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : в„•) : Prop :=
  вҲғ (f : Fin k вҶӘ V)
    (paths : вҲҖ (i j : Fin k), i < j вҶ’ G.Walk (f i) (f j)),
    (вҲҖ i j (h : i < j), (paths i j h).IsPath) вҲ§
    (вҲҖ iвӮҒ jвӮҒ (hвӮҒ : iвӮҒ < jвӮҒ) iвӮӮ jвӮӮ (hвӮӮ : iвӮӮ < jвӮӮ),
      (iвӮҒ, jвӮҒ) вү  (iвӮӮ, jвӮӮ) вҶ’
      вҲҖ v, v вҲҲ (paths iвӮҒ jвӮҒ hвӮҒ).support.tail.dropLast вҶ’
           v вҲү (paths iвӮӮ jвӮӮ hвӮӮ).support.tail.dropLast) вҲ§
    (вҲҖ i j (h : i < j) v,
      v вҲҲ (paths i j h).support.tail.dropLast вҶ’
      вҲҖ m : Fin k, v вү  f m)

/--
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 718** (KomlГіsвҖ“SzemerГ©di [KoSz96], BollobГЎsвҖ“Thomason [BoTh96]):

There exists an absolute constant $C > 0$ such that any graph on $n$ vertices
with at least $C \cdot r^2 \cdot n$ edges contains a subdivision of $K_r$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_718 :
    вҲғ C : в„қ, C > 0 вҲ§
      вҲҖ (n r : в„•),
        вҲҖ (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
          (G.edgeFinset.card : в„қ) вүҘ C * (r : в„қ) ^ 2 * (n : в„қ) вҶ’
          ContainsSubdivision G r := by
  sorry

end Erdos718

content.text: The review summary says: "The file is fully compliant with all applicable checklist items." The only unchecked items are build verification (requires running `lake build`) and an optional suggestion. The review passed вҖ” no changes needed.
